description: Got a bunch of apache logs at /home/user/logs/access.log — need to pull out just the IP addresses and response codes, tab-separated, one per line. Like `192.168.1.1	200` format. Dump it to /home/user/logs/ip_status.txt.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/logs/access.log exists with ~50 lines of Combined Log Format entries, e.g.:
    192.168.1.45 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:23:15 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
    10.0.0.12 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:23:16 +0000] "POST /api/login HTTP/1.1" 401 567 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
    172.16.5.99 - - [15/Jan/2024:10:23:17 +0000] "GET /favicon.ico HTTP/1.1" 404 209 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
- IP is always field 1. Status code is always field 9 in standard Combined Log Format (space-delimited, quoted request string counts as one field when using awk with default FS, but the quotes complicate naive splitting — however the status code follows the closing quote of the request).
- /home/user/logs/ is writable. /home/user/logs/ip_status.txt does not exist initially.
- awk, sed, grep, cut available. Standard coreutils.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/logs/ip_status.txt exists.
- Each line is exactly: IP<tab>STATUS_CODE (e.g., "192.168.1.45\t200").
- Number of lines in ip_status.txt equals number of lines in access.log.
- IPs match field 1 of each access.log line; status codes match the HTTP response code (the number after "HTTP/1.1" and the closing quote).

Invariants:
- /home/user/logs/access.log unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- ip_status.txt must have the same line count as access.log.
- Every IP in ip_status.txt must appear in the corresponding line of access.log (positional match).
- Every status code must be a 3-digit number matching the actual response code from that log line.
name: task_000000_0ea7be88
